Layer 4: Frame parts, AB+extruder motors, bearings, a BMG gear set and the hotend. Note that Formbot included aluminium 64T pulleys for the Z-drives which is an upgrade from the default printed ones! There's also some DIN rails which are optional on the 120, but required on the 180 Micron.
These are the polymer spherical bearings which I'm running fine on my other Micron and on my V2.4, as well as the MGN9H X-rail.

Mainboard - BTT Manta M8p, without it's carton, probably because that one's kinda big. But jumpers and the important rubber ducky are included! I built my first Micron with this board and it is alright for almost all your printing needs.

Also note: They included spherical bearings for the gantry - in my opinion something that should be standard not only on the Micron, but the V2.4 too, and the X-rail is MGN9H, which means that you can upgrade the kit to a Boop probe without having to source a new linear rail.
